desc:
Four stone pillars support the domed ceiling of Moria's eastern gate. A great
arch frames the impending darkness to the west. The two large bronze gates are
nicked and marred in ancient battle. To the east can be seen a lush dale, the
fragrance of pine and wildflowers fall in stark contrast to the dank humidity of
the caves. The north and south yield sheer drops onto a talus covered field.
Below, a wide marble staircase leads to the broken road of Durin's way.
name: The Great Gates

desc:
To the east, housed in a great arch and flanked by huge doorposts, lie the great
eastern gates of Khazad-dum. There are several shafts cut in the east- ern wall
above the gates, so this area is flooded with daylight whenever the sun shines.
To the north lies what must be a guardroom, though it is impos- sible to tell if
the current inhabitants of Moria make use of it. Orcs must come here sometimes,
however, for the evidence of their crude attempts to block the shafts with
debris lies scattered about.
name: Hall of the Eastern Gate

desc:
To the east, the high ceiling plunges abruptly toward the floor, forming a stone
wall from which hang some broken stone doors. The wall above the doors is
pierced with several great shafts which open onto the mountainside, and fresh
air pours from them down into the passage. This area seems to have been shunned
by the orcs, probably because of the light that must pour through the shafts
whenever the sun rises above Lothlorien and the Anduin to the east. To the west,
the darkness beckons with promises of secret treasure hidden in the ancient
Dwarven mansions.
name: First Hall of Moria

desc:
The great hall running from east to west is met here by a smaller passage that
leads north. East and west, the roof soars high above, supported by lofty arches
seemingly too slender to support the mountain above them. The air is almost
fresh here, lacking the stale musty quality of underground areas that have been
infested with orcs.
name: First Hall of Moria

desc:
The First Hall ends here in a broad stair leading down to the west. To the east
it runs in quiet splendour, for the most part unused by the orcs. The ceiling is
very high here, and there seems to be a slight breeze from the east gambolling
among the lofty arches.
name: First Hall of Moria

desc:
These grand stairs lead upward to the east. They are very wide, and have been
cut in such a way that both dwarves and larger races can use them with little
discomfort. At the foot of the stairs is a great door, built for defensive
purposes by the dwarves in their heyday. At the top of the stairs to the east a
wide hall begins, presumably leading to the eastern gate.
name: Broad Stair

desc:
Crude lice-infested pallets are scattered about, all currently unoccupied, but
obviously frequently used. This area is home to the orcs unfortunate enough to
be assigned the duty of guarding the eastern gate, for the shafts in the ceiling
frequently fill these halls with bright sunlight. To the south lies the gateroom
itself, and the exit to the Dimrill Dale and the lands beyond. Apparently this
area is not well guarded, for since the great battle of Azan- ulbizar no one has
brought an army strong enough to pose a serious threat to the great evil that
lies deep underneath the mountains.
name: Chamber of the Eastern Guard

desc:
Lined with huge blocks of dark black rock, this passage leaves a major hall to
the south and goes into the darkness to the north. There is a thick coating of
dust resting on every surface -- obviously this place is not frequently visited.
name: Outer Passage

desc:
The passage turns to the east here, becoming slightly narrower. The blocks that
form the walls and floor are made of a dark basalt-like substance, and have
somehow been welded together almost seamlessly. The passage opens into a chamber
a short distance to the east, and this whole area has the appearance of having
been long abandoned.
name: Outer Passage

desc:
Its purpose lost in antiquity, this room has lain abandoned for many years. The
passage to the west is made of large blocks of black stone so cunningly crafted
that the joints between contiguous blocks are barely discernible. A similar
passage leads north, but it appears to quickly fill with rubble. From the east
an unpleasant odour is carried on a slight breeze -- there seems to be another
chamber in that direction.
name: Small Chamber

desc:
For a short distance it is possible to squeeze between the heavy blocks that
have collapsed inward from the walls, but after a little ways all space is taken
by the debris. Perhaps this passage once led to more chambers to the north, but
for now they must remain lost.
name: Choked Passage

desc:
A significant portion of the ceiling of this high-domed room has collapsed,
revealing an irregularly shaped patch of open sky on the other side of the short
passage created by the mishap. The pile of rubble on the floor is too short to
allow one to exit through the tunnel without aid of wings, but the quantity of
feathers and faeces coating the debris indicate that this room is shelter for
several foul birds, content to live in their own filth.
name: Small Dome

desc:
This flat area was part of the Dwarven defensive scheme should their mansions
ever be invaded from the eastern gate. The imposing door to the east was built
of reinforced iron to resist battering rams, and even if it should ever be
compromised, the dwarves would simply retreat to the west, destroying the narrow
bridge behind them. Any enemies trapped on this side would then be exposed to
merciless crossbow fire from the opposite side.
name: A Great Hall

desc:
This extremely slender bridge was constructed by the dwarves as a last defence
should the Eastern Gate and First Hall of Moria be taken. In a single great
spring of fifty feet, the narrow stone bridge arcs over the abyss. No guardrails
are present to prevent the careless adventurer misstepping and plummeting to his
death in the yawning darkness.
name: Durin's Bridge

desc:
Two parallel lines of large pillars march down the centre of this major road,
continuing as far as can be seen to the west. To the east, a wide chasm cuts
across the road, bridged by a single slender bridge of stone. The road marches
right to the edge of the chasm -- there are no signs of guardrails -- so it is
possible to peer over the edge into the blackness.
name: A Great Hall

desc:
Running down the centre of this magnificent hallway are two parallel rows of
carved pillars, stretching both east and west in perfect rank and file. The
floor here is made of a glossy black stone -- as are the pillars -- and any
light is dully reflected off of the exposed rock. There is little dust here, so
apparently this hall is frequently in use by the current inhabitants of the
depths. To the north is a low archway, and beyond is another hall.
name: A Great Hall

desc:
This is a large avenue that stretches from the Gate of Durin to the Dimrill
Gate. It is a very large, arched tunnel and is constructed of grey granite and
white marble. Littered across the floor are broken weapons, armour, and bone
fragments. Inhuman corpses lay rotting along the north wall in a pool of blood.
It appears that there was a battle here recently.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
Directly to the south is a low archway, and through it can be seen a major
passage -- surely one of the major arteries of Khazad-dum. The keystone of the
arch bears a small inscription -- still legible but faded with time -- that
labels the road to the south 'Durin's Way'. To the north, the passage continues,
but darkness precludes any details from being ascertained.
name: Before a Low Archway

desc:
Another passageway once intersected this one here, but earthquakes or other
tumults have caused it to collapse so it only possible to continue north. The
north-south passage is still relatively clear of rubble, but whether that is due
to blind chance or superior construction cannot be determined.
name: A Passageway

desc:
This passage is worn, but it does not appear to be used at all by the orcs that
inhabit this area of Moria. A coating of dust and small cobwebs covers the
floor, and from time to time small spiders dart across the floor and quickly
scurry back into cracks in the masonry.
name: A Passageway

desc:
This nondescript passageway turns south to east here. To the east much of the
passage has already succumbed to the passage of time, and the accumu- lated
rubble would make progress in that direction difficult. To the south the passage
is clear of debris. The walls of this passage are covered with flat slate panels
-- fairly thin judging by the many panels that have fallen to the east.
name: A Passageway

desc:
According to the knowledge passed on through generations of dwarves since the
halls of Moria were abandoned, a great many dwarves made their homes in this
area of Khazad-dum. These dwelling places have now been sealed off from the
outside world, as the passage east has been totally destroyed -- perhaps in the
tumults following the awakening of Durin's Bane, or perhaps deliberately brought
down by malicious vandals.
name: Collapsed Passage

desc:
This is a large avenue that stretches all the way from the Gate of Durin to the
Dimrill Gate. It is a very large tunnel, arched, and constructed of grey granite
and white marble. The lanterns, hanging from rings in the ceiling, are broken
and useless.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
This is a huge octagonal chamber with smooth, slime-covered walls of glittering
basalt and black obsidian. The ceiling rises to a shallow peak and sits on two
rows of massive lamp-shaped pillars. At the centre of each pillar is an enormous
lamp, wrought of gold and silver, which are now broken and burned. There is a
very musty odour in this room, and the floor is so damp it is almost slippery.
An odd well occupies the centre of the room.
name: The Central Gathering Hall

desc:
This wide arched hallway branches south from the large hall to the north. Dank
and musty the granite hall is covered in filth and garbage. Kicked to the side
of the hall, broken weapons and other scrapes of wood lie forgotten, while the
middle of the hall is fairly clear of debris.
name: Granite Hallway

desc:
The granite hallway continues to the north and south while a smaller hallway
breaks off to the east. The air is dank and musty. Slime and dirt form a fairly
thick casing over the granite walls and more garbage and broken weapons clutter
the floor along the eastern wall. The rest of the passage is disgustingly
filthy.
name: Granite Hallway

desc:
This wide, arched tunnel is carved from grey granite. Blood stains the floor and
the skeletal remains of some hapless victim lie piled in the corner. The tall
and plain grey walls continue to the north and south.
name: Granite Hallway

desc:
This circular chamber is lined with pallets for seating - presumably it is some
sort of gathering place. The place has a musty smell, and it feels damp. Several
species of fungus seem to have taken advantage of the favourable conditions and
flourished here.
name: Chamber of Meditation

desc:
A guardroom lies just to the north, while to the east a larger room of some sort
opens up. The passage is dank, and in many places fungi have found purchase in
nooks and crannies in the walls. The passageway leading west seems to be but a
continuation of this one.
name: A Musty Hallway

desc:
This narrow passageway becomes decidedly damper to the east. In that direction,
the walls have been invaded by lichen and fungi that have begun the task of
crumbling the quarried stone. To the west, the passage is dry, but nothing more
can be made out.
name: A Musty Hallway

desc:
To the north is a small chamber, probably used to house orkish guards. To the
west, the passage opens into a large room that appears to be filled with round,
ivory objects. To the east, the passageway continues, becoming dank and musty
with each passing step.
name: A Musty Hallway

desc:
This room is filled with the cleanly polished skulls of dozens of orcs. The
bones have been bleached to a pristine white, and runes of brilliant crimson
have been carefully inscribed in the centre of the low, sloping foreheads. The
skulls seem to have been deliberately placed -- there is none of the usual
haphazard scattering associated with orcs -- so perhaps they have some
ritualistic function.
name: Chamber of Skulls

desc:
Cracked marble slabs line this corridor, which appears to have been damaged by
an explosion of great force. To the east is an old Dwarven guardroom, perhaps
still used by the orcs, while to the south is a chamber of some type.
name: A Marble Tunnel

desc:
The walls here are covered by a thin layer of dark soot that diminishes the
beauty with which they once shone. In the few places where the soot has been
brushed away, white marble banded with ribbons of polished grey granite are
revealed.
name: A Marble Tunnel

desc:
This is a wide and arched tunnel, constructed of white marble with bands of grey
granite. The walls have been blackened by soot -- it appears to be deliberate --
and smeared in the black ash are crude runes of burning red.
name: A Marble Tunnel

desc:
This is a huge square chamber with smooth, glimmering walls of diamond-
patterned basalt and black obsidian. The ceiling rises to a shallow peak and
sits on two lines of massive tree-shaped pillars. Crystal lamps, now blackened
and broken, hang from the high ceiling. Bones and broken weapons are piled up
against the pillars.
name: Western Gathering Hall

desc:
This is a wide tunnel carved from milky white marble, stained with blood and
ashes. Several rows of skulls are lined up on top of the pipes adorning the
walls. The tunnel continues to the north and opens up into a wide hall to the
south.
name: A Marble Tunnel

desc:
This is a wide tunnel lined with large slabs of white marble. Some of the once
bright slabs have fallen into the corridor, shattering on the floor. In the
cavities revealed by the fallen marble, a series of ancient pipes can be seen.
The tunnel continues to the north and south.
name: A Marble Tunnel

desc:
This wide tunnel was once lined with milky-white marble that now has mostly
cracked and fallen away. A series of pipes have been revealed behind the marble
facade. The tunnel continues to the south, while opening into a larger chamber
to the north. There is also a doorway to the east.
name: A Marble Tunnel

desc:
This is an enormous room set with a high arching, rib-vaulted ceiling adorned
with gold filigree and silver leaf. There are seven wide white marble columns
spaced throughout the room. Ten rows of obsidian-lined niches ring the walls at
even intervals, each of which house a bronze lamp. This room, now burned and its
lamps broken, has an evil feel to it.
name: A Ceremonial Chamber

desc:
The tunnel is wide, arched and constructed from band-patterned grey granite and
white marble. The battered walls are cracked and weak from whatever battle took
place here. Bits of rock and dirt fall from the ceiling and you don't know if it
will cave in or not. A series of pipes lines the cracked walls.
name: A Crumbling Tunnel

desc:
This wide, arched tunnel is constructed of grey granite and white marble woven
in a band pattern. Stones and dirt fall from the decaying ceiling and walls into
piles on the floor. Occasionally you can hear the sounds of the walls settling,
and it seems as if the tunnel will collapse at any minute. A set of pipes runs
along the walls.
name: A Crumbling Tunnel

desc:
This wide, arched tunnel is constructed of grey granite and white marble. The
walls are battered and cracked from some great battle fought here. The sounds of
the walls creaking and settling make you wary of a cave-in. Stones and dust fall
from the ceiling and settle into piles on the floor. The tunnel continues to the
west or opens into a large hall to the east. There appears to be some kind of
guard room to the south.
name: A Crumbling Tunnel

desc:
This is a huge rectangular chamber with smooth, glimmering walls of diamond-
patterned basalt and black obsidian. The ceiling rises to shallow peaks and sits
on two lines of tree-shaped or lamp-shaped pillars. A set of huge crystal
chandeliers hang from the ceiling, but they are broken and burned.
name: A Gathering Hall

desc:
This is a spiral stairwell connecting this level of the city to those above and
below it. The stairs, cut shallow to suit the travel of dwarves, are awkward for
most people to travel.
name: A Stairwell

desc:
This is a wide tunnel cut from jet-black ebony. The walls seem to absorb what
little light you can produce. There is an ominous sense of evil here and you
want to leave. The hallway opens into a large chamber to the north and continues
to the south. There is a guard chamber to the west. A series of pipes lines the
walls.
name: An Ebony Hallway

desc:
This is a wide tunnel cut from jet-black rock. Although once they must have been
polished to a lustrous sheen, now the walls seem to absorb whatever light is
present. The tunnel continues north and south and a smaller passage opens up to
the east. West of here a larger chamber can be seen, and from this direction the
unmistakable odour of trolls can be detected.
name: An Ebony Hallway

desc:
This wide tunnel of cut from black rock leads north from the great passage to
the south. To the west is an old Dwarven guardroom -- perhaps used by the orcs
for the same purpose. Once these walls would have been kept polished until they
gleamed like polished ebony, but now the dull black colour simply seems to
absorb all light.
name: An Ebony Hallway

desc:
This nondescript chamber houses a portion of the Burzruth militia. There are
three straw pallets in the back of the room - crawling with ticks by the looks
of them. The guardroom has an excellent view of the passageways to the south and
west.
name: Guard Chamber

desc:
This is the famous Durin's Way which passes through the heart of the mountain.
There are several solid stone blocks scattered here amid smaller piles of rocks
and rubble, perhaps due to a small cave-in which occurred not long ago. Heavy
ironwood beams have been used in a slip-shod attempt to shore up some of the
walls near here, but they appear to be quite ineffective in preventing further
collapse. Durin's Way continues east and west, but your attention is especially
drawn to a sturdy stonedoor in the north wall here.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
Durin's Way continues to progress both east and west through the stony heart of
Caradhras. The roadway is in good condition here and the air seems fresh. The
Dwarves of Moria must have spent years of effort carving the surrounding stone
with great precision to create this perfect passageway.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
Durin's Way is blocked here by a large barricade, complete with a crude door of
obvious orkish manufacture. A large wooden table lies against the wall to the
north, and several pallets have been placed along the southern wall. This place
perhaps serves as a guardpost of some kind.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
The Avenue of Iron comes to an abrupt end in a most unexpected manner here. It
runs directly east to the base of a massive solid steel wall which completely
blocks any passage to or from the east. You search every inch of the wall and
find no secret doors or keyholes, but do notice several points where the wall
seems to have been dented from the other side! What force did this wall serve to
hold back? Some mysteries are perhaps better left unsolved. The Avenue leads
back west from here.
name: Avenue of Iron

desc:
This place is now so dark that you feel smothered, as if trapped in a giant
black fist which is slowly crushing all within it. You inch along to the east
past some huge blocks of dark stone and come upon an obstacle that you cannot
surmount: a colossal pile of rubble hundreds of feet high which completely fills
the Avenue. It would take an army of Dwarves a month to clear a path through
this mess. There is no other choice but to travel west from here.
name: Bronze Avenue

desc:
A large stairway winds down from above, pauses at a landing, and then continues
downward. To the north and south, rooms that were surely used to station guards
flank the wide hallway. East and west, Durin's Way continues its journey beneath
the mountains.
name: Mustering Stairs

desc:
This is a large Avenue that stretches from the Gates of Durin to the Dimrill
gate. It is a very large tunnel, arched, and constructed of a band-pattern weave
of grey granite and white marble. There are broken lanterns hanging from rings
in the ceiling.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
This is a large Avenue that stretches from the westernmost part of Khazad-dum to
the Dimrill Gates. It is a very large tunnel, arched, and constructed of grey
granite and white marble. The lanterns that should light the passage hang broken
and useless from the ceiling.
name: Durin's Way

desc:
You fall without concept of time, beyond all light and deep into the earth.
Eventually, your body is broken on a jagged rock wall, before it is plunged into
icy-cold water to rot.
name: The Chasm of Moria

desc:
You fall without concept of time, beyond all light and deep into the earth.
Eventually, your body is broken on a jagged rock wall, before it is plunged into
icy-cold water to rot.
name: The Chasm of Moria

desc:
This passageway is about half the size of the Avenues running through
Khazad-dum. The walls and ceiling are smooth and flat, bearing no decorations or
inscriptions. Broken torch-brackets line the walls.
name: A Short Passageway

desc:
This armoury lies quiet and empty - abandoned now for hundreds of years. There
is hardly a sign left from the days when the dwarves inhabited this place. The
weapons and armour that were once stockpiled here were plundered or destroyed
long ago.
name: Abandoned Armoury

desc:
This is a small guard room used by the guards to watch over the Central
Gathering Hall. This lower room is where guards stand ready to run out and crush
whatever opponent may be in the Hall. This room is filthy and there is a rotting
corpse in the corner. There is a window in the northeast corner of the room
looking into the Hall.
name: A Guard Room

desc:
This is a small room where guards can stand watch over the Western Gathering
Hall, ready to rush out and crush whatever enemy may be there. There is a window
in the northwest corner looking into the Hall. Bones lay strewn about the room
and there is a rotting corpse in the middle of the room.
name: Lower Guard Room

desc:
This small room is the lower room used by armed guards, who stand watch over the
Central Gathering Hall, ready to rush out and crush whatever enemy may enter.
The chamber is filthy, and a heap of bones lies in one corner, whilst in the
opposite corner a window looks out into the hall.
name: A Guard Room

desc:
This small chamber serves as a guard room, home to those who stand watch over
the Western Gathering Hall, ready to rush out and mercilessly crush whatever
enemy who may be foolish enough to enter. A large window in the southwest corner
of the room looks out onto the hall, affording a clear view, and piles of bones
lie strewn all about.
name: Lower Guard Room

desc:
This is a small room where guards can keep watch over the Ceremonial Hall. There
is a small window in the northwest corner of the room and a row of arrow slits
where guards can use bows to support their comrades if there is trouble in the
Hall. This room is filthy, and there is a pile of bones in the corner that
probably belonged to the Dwarves who first owned this city.
name: Guard Chamber

desc:
This is a small guard room used to keep watch over the Gathering Hall. There is
a small window and a number of arrow slits in the northeast corner of the room.
This room is filthy and a stench of death seems to hang over this place. There
is a pile of bones in the corner.
name: Guard Chamber

desc:
This passage quickly ends in a wall of rubble to the west. The small portion
that has survived looks like it was once very elegant -- somehow pristine white
marble and some type of glossy black basalt were made to seamlessly melt into
one another, creating a striking contrast on the floors and walls. A small
inscribed plaque on the northern wall still bears some runes.
name: Dead-end Passage

desc:
These wide stairs quickly narrow and begin to spiral up through the shaft above.
The stairs lack ornamentation of any sort, as does the corridor in which they
terminate, suggesting that perhaps this area was not for public use. A small
bronze plaque, upon which runes are visible, is prominently displayed on the
wall next to the stairs.
name: Foot of Stair

desc:
The passage is dim and barren of all else except rock. The plain grey granite
walls run east and west into darkness. There is a small opening to the north.
name: Small Avenue

desc:
The air is stale and still. A layer of dust has settled onto everything in this
small room. A small table lies overturned on the ground and a small little stool
stands upturned in the corner. The granite walls of the room are solid except
for the small opening to the south.
name: Abandoned Quarters

desc:
This passage is cut from dull grey granite. The air is stale and still in the
darkness. Here the rocky corridor bends so that it runs west and south. Bits of
rusted metal and bones here and there litter the sides of the passage. Settled
dust and rock debris lie on the passage floor.
name: Small Avenue

desc:
Here three paths converge from the north, east, and west. The ground here is
scattered with bits of debris which mostly collect in the corners of the room.
To the west a room opens up while hallways continue to the north and east.
name: The Junction

desc:
This hallway is cut out of solid grey granite as the sides of the alley seem to
indicate. The way seems to curve to the west while continuing to the north.
name: Small Alley

desc:
The alley appears to have been carved out of solid grey granite as the sides and
ceiling are made of the same rock. The ground, however, is littered with bits
and scraps of dusty junk which gathers to the side of this hallway which runs
north and south. A strong stench emanates from the north.
name: Small Alley

desc:
The room is strewn with ragged bits of cloth resembling blankets and numerous
charred wooden frames hint of Dwarven beds. However nothing in the room appears
to have been left in one piece as orcs have probably pillaged their way here
once.
name: Abandoned Barracks

desc:
Pieces of tables and cracked little stools sprawled all over are evidence that
this may have once been a dining area. Cracked bits of plates and rusted spoons
litter the floor. A dark little room is visible to the east while there is a
larger opening to the north.
name: Mess Hall

desc:
This appears to have once been a food storeroom used by the dwarves. Having been
ransacked and looted by marauding orcs, there seems to be nothing left save for
bits of shattered barrels and some tattered bits of flour sacks. Mould and
yellow rotten flour cover the ground. A small archway to the west appears to be
the only exit.
name: A Supply Room

desc:
This dank cell is used to hold prisoners that are to be sacrificed to whatever
dark god these orcs worship - more than likely Durin's Bane. The accommodations
are spartan, there is not even a pile of straw to sleep on. The previous
occupants of the cell soiled the back corner with their filth, so the stench is
horrible.
name: Cell

desc:
This dark room has had its walls painted black and inscribed with strange runes
of flaming crimson. In one corner are the remains of a fire -- the grey ashes
have been tracked around the room. On the walls are several shelves stocked with
paraphernalia of unknown uses. A straw pallet lies in the southwest corner.
name: Shaman's Chamber

desc:
The Durghash guards use this small room for sleeping, gambling, and most of all,
arguing. They are currently gathered about a small table in the centre of the
room with a set of rolling bones on it. Most look very unhappy to you.
name: Guards' Quarters

desc:
This Dwarven guard-room has apparently been appropriated by an orc of some
importance for use as both living quarters and command post. The ruined cushions
shoved in one corner provide a place to sleep, and the large table in the centre
of the room is used for both meals -- judging by the remains upon it -- and as a
workspace.
name: Guard-post

desc:
The stairs are in bad shape here and many of the individual steps have been
pitted and scarred. Even the shaft through which the staircase spirals pos-
sesses cracks and fractures in abundance. Below, the spiralling stairs cease
their winding, and become a wide and broad stair leading down and south.
name: Hidden Stair

desc:
The stairs here are slightly damaged -- each exposed surface is pitted, as if an
enormous force had hurled sand and small pebbles at the rock. Above, the stairs
appear to end in a corridor of some type, while below the steps disappear
quickly into the night.
name: Hidden Stair

desc:
This is a small room where guards can keep watch over the Gathering Hall. There
is a small window in the southeast corner of the room and a row of arrow slits
allowing archers to fire arrows at enemies in the Hall. In the centre of the
room is a spear mounted in the floor. Spitted on that spear about halfway up, is
an unfortunate goblin, still twitching in agony.
name: Guard Chamber

desc:
This chamber appears to be some sort of guardpost. It contains a large table and
a couple of chairs, but it seems not to have been used for some time. A small
dark tunnel leads into the north from here, and a large opening has been smashed
through the wall to the west.
name: A Chamber

desc:
This is a simple tunnel with a high roof and rough-carved walls that appear to
be recently worked. There is a putrid odour in the air here that calls to mind
images of meat left to spoil in the open. The simple tunnel continues north, but
opens up into a chamber just to the south.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
A fairly large tunnel bends east to south from here. Strange grunting sounds and
an evil smell come to you from somewhere to the east, but the south is just dark
and silent. Looking toward one small side of the niche one can see what might be
the corpse of a small bat.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
This gigantic cavern is occupied by some cavetrolls, and it smells awful! The
foul, dank air immediately invades your lungs like an unseen enemy, burning your
eyes and throat. The awful stench actually originates from somewhere to the
north, but the inhabitants of this cavern certainly add to the problem. This
place is richly furnished with filthy strawmats, broken stone slabs and plenty
of decaying corpses scattered about the area. Trolls surely find it much to
their liking, and they seem eager to welcome you into their home. When you
decide to leave, you can flee in any of four directions.
name: A Huge Cavern

desc:
This cave is apparently used as both a dumping ground and source of water for
the trolls who inhabit this area. There is a small stream trickling through the
back of the cavern, but it is almost clogged with the accumulated filth of the
trolls.
name: A Smelly Cavern

desc:
This is a filthy and disgusting troll den! The air is ripe with the stench of
trolls, and there are quite a few tall, pale cavetrolls waiting in here. You see
many bones and colourful rags lying all over the cave floor. The only obvious
exit leads back into the huge cavern to the north.
name: A Small Cavern

desc:
The tunnel bends from west to south here. There are loud grunting sounds coming
from the west, and this should arouse both fear and caution in any who would
travel that way.
name: Bend in the Large Tunnel

desc:
You are travelling in a broad, well-made tunnel that leads both north and south.
The walls and floor of this tunnel are smoother than most others you have seen
here, and the tunnel seems to run in a very straight line.
name: A Large Tunnel

desc:
This tunnel appears to be freshly excavated and is in good shape with no risk of
collapse. The walls and floor are not smooth, but the tunnel itself is very
straight. You can go north or south from here.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
This tunnel seems to have been recently excavated, though not with dwarven skill
and attention to detail. The walls and floor are not smooth, but they appear to
be in good shape and there is little risk of collapse. The tunnel itself is very
straight and leads due north and south from this point.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
This is an extremely small passage, barely big enough for you to fit into
without stooping over. It is dark in here and you cannot make out the finer
details of your surroundings. The darkness seems to become almost absolute to
the south.
name: A Small, Dark Passage

desc:
Here a small, dark passage leading north reaches a deadend at a large square
hole in the floor. The hole appears to open into unfathomable darkness, and it
could be either twenty feet or twenty thousand to the bottom.
name: By the Hole

desc:
This room is more than simply large, it is positively gigantic. The ceiling
rises at least thirty feet above your head, high enough to accommodate any
creature that you have ever encountered. Huge, broken stone steps climb the
south wall to a large opening in the ceiling, and you cannot help noticing that
each wall is pigmented with a distinct colour. There is a huge bed in here as
well, and this serves to harden suspicions as to who or rather what occupies
this place.
name: The Large Room

desc:
A very strange tunnel indeed! The walls and ceiling here are in such poor
condition that you are now certain that work here was abandoned in haste long
ago. Your eyes pick out some dark patches clustered on one of the walls here
which, for some reason, brings to mind the gore resulting from a slaughtered
beast. The tunnel is completely silent and pitch black, and continues south.
name: A Strange Tunnel

desc:
This unfinished tunnel bends sharply west and north. You find many old mining
tools scattered about here, but all are cracked and pitted by the weight of many
centuries of disuse. The builders of this tunnel must have been in a great hurry
to finish here, or maybe they were suddenly forced to retreat from this place.
You see no signs of a battle here, but through some trick of the mind you
imagine hearing the deathcries of terrified dwarves dying in great agony.
name: A Strange Tunnel

desc:
This 'cave' is really nothing more than the junction of three large tunnels. The
rough stonework exhibited here indicates that work on these tunnels was never
properly completed and the fossilised mining equipment scattered about here
arouses your curiosity even more. Exactly what occurred here and when? Dark
tunnels branch off to the east and west, and another smaller passageway plunges
into absolute blackness to the south.
name: A Cave

desc:
The walls and roof here are jagged and quite irregular, rather slipshop
workmanship for Moria dwarves. Perhaps they were pressed for time when they
worked here. An unexpected heaviness in the air conjures thoughts of great evil
lurking somewhere nearby, and you suddenly feel as though you should be moving
along too. The passage continues north and east.
name: A Strange Tunnel

desc:
From this point, this curious tunnel continues back to the south. The passageway
here is only rough-hewn from the surrounding rock which is black as pitch and
cold to the touch. It seems as though whoever made this tunnel worked in great
haste and did not take the care to craft the stonework up to the usual standard.
name: A Strange Tunnel

desc:
Here the tunnel comes to an abrupt deadend in absolute darkness. Work seems to
be in progress here, perhaps in hope of progressing farther into the bedrock
under the mountain. The obvious exit is back to the north, but your quick eyes
catch sight of some strange scratches made in the dirt here.
name: The Sudden Deadend

desc:
This is a tiny passage hewn from the rock as a maintenance passageway. The walls
are rough and the passage is not perfectly straight like the other passages in
Khazad-dum. The layer of dust on the floor suggests that this place has not seen
any visitors for a long time.
name: An Accessway

desc:
This tiny passageway was hewn from the rock for maintenance reasons. The
rough-hewn passage is not perfectly straight like the other passages in
Khazad-dum. The layer of dust on the floor suggests that this place has not seen
any visitors for a long time.
name: An Accessway

desc:
This small passage was cut through the rock as a maintenance passageway. There
used to be a vertical shaft in the middle of the room leading up and down, with
iron rings hanging from its sides; but the passage below is filled with rubble,
and the rings above are so rusty that they would no longer support one's weight.
name: An Accessway

desc:
This dreadful cavern is used as a throne room by a great troll chieftain. A
great throne is set in the north wall, adorned with the skulls of his victims.
Piles of bones are set in the corners of the room, and bloodstains cover the
walls and floor.
name: Throneroom

desc:
This hall has been excavated around the landing of a once-magnificent curving
staircase. From the centre of the polished marble floor of the hall's west end,
stone stairs rise to the west, then boldly vault upwards and toward the north as
if unsupported and disappear behind an ornate screen in the northwest corner of
the ceiling. In the centre of the east end of the hall, steps descend in a bold
curve, east, then south; at the point where they disappear the sheer drop from
the landing to the steps is about thirty yards. Along the walls are carved
ornate lampstands. The ceiling, 90 feet above or more, is ornately decorated and
inlaid with stones of many colours. But their beauty today is hidden behind
layers of dust and cobwebs; and near the ground, the delicate carvings are
smashed, the sturdier ones are chipped, and all lies strewn with foul debris.
Archways leave this hall to the north and south.
name: Stairwell

desc:
A broad, high-ceilinged underground avenue is met at this point by a short
flight of steps, fully twenty yards wide, which leads upward and eastward into a
forest of columns. While the avenue apparently once carried the main flow of
traffic north and south, the northward way has by now been almost completely
choked by rubble and debris.
name: Underground Entranceway

desc:
Here the underground avenue turns upward and to the east, where it is abruptly
blocked by a gigantic grim iron portcullis. A heavy metal post is lying here
which has been used as a ram in an attempt to break it down, and someone has
also attempted to excavate the stone under the portcullis. The Dwarven iron and
the dark lustrous roadbed have resisted these crude efforts.
name: Bend in the Avenue

desc:
You are in a hall whose high ceiling is supported by a forest of fluted columns
spaced more closely together than usual. Lines of columns march away from you
into the darkness in the directions of the cardinal points. To the north and
south the ceiling bows down near to the floor and touches the top rank of a low
tier of benches. This much is clearly Dwarven work; but the food troughs which
have been placed near the centre of the room, the small bone fragments to be
found everywhere, and the stench of rotting meat and worse are the work of the
later occupants of this underground city.
name: Assembly Hall

desc:
The stench of death fills this once-handsome chamber. Perfectly proportioned,
its walls, floor, and ceiling are marble of the purest white, carved in great
slabs and assembled here. At the four corners are pedestals which once held
great lampstands; perhaps the lamps were made of a valuable material, since they
have been taken away. On a raised stone dais is a small seat, made of the same
stone as the rest of the chamber. Surrounding it, corpses are arrayed on the
floor. Some have been butchered; some appear to have been butchered alive. Some
of the grislier carcasses have been wired to metal frameworks and seem to have
been a long time dying, and then a long time dead. Most are orcs, but you
believe you can recognise an elf and a dwarf, though not much is left of them.
name: Corpse Pit

desc:
This small passage is lined by false columns of brilliant white marble and of a
light blue stone. The stone is soiled and stained now, and foul writings and
drawings have been scrawled on the walls. To the east the passage is terminated
abruptly by a rockfall, and the walls around it are cracked and black with soot.
The passage opens into a larger hall to the west, and another room opens to the
south.
name: Narrow Tunnel

desc:
This chamber is about thirty feet across and carved in the shape of a
hemisphere. The inner surface of the dome was carved and polished so as to
display the colours of a vein of marbled quartz. Around the walls is a
continuous low stone bench, and in the centre of the room is a hearth. A cool
breeze from an unseen vent airs the room. This may once have been a Dwarvish
place of contemplation, but it has been turned to other purposes. The hearth,
which has been recently used, stinks of ash and burned fat, and is choked with
burned hair, skin, and bones. Vats and pots of thick liquid, pigments of black
and red, are arranged around it.
name: Painting Chamber

desc:
Once this dark passageway was a truly magnificent hallway. The walls on either
side are carved in bas-reliefs inlaid in many-coloured stones. Dwarven lords
bearing the riches of Khazad-dum marched northward at stately pace in these
carvings. The pattern of the gouges and pits which have been made in the stone
suggest that their axe-hilts, their bracelets, their glittering eyes were
depicted using real gold, gems, and truesilver. Nothing remains of this, of
course.
name: Hallway

desc:
This is an octagonal underground hall about 100 yards across with a false
ceiling twenty yards high. The false ceiling is actually made of narrow
criss-crossing metal beams ornamented with white marble and supporting a
multitude of lamps carved in crystalline forms. Very low benches surround a
central fountain. All walls but the west are smoothly polished, but the west
wall is unadorned and rough. In its centre a low tunnel leads into the
blackness.
name: Underground Plaza

desc:
This east-west passage seems to be an undistinguished mine tunnel, long
abandoned. However, midway along its length, a line of heavy marble blocks about
15 inches in height has been placed along the south wall; here also the north
wall has been polished flat, and some lines of runes carved carefully in the
rock.
name: Abandoned Passage

desc:
A broad underground avenue extends to the north, and at one time must have
continued to the south, descending slightly. However, the southward passage is
completely blocked, as the passageway is choked with mud and sand. From a broad
transverse crack in the ceiling, a curtain of water smelling of foul rot drips
mournfully, making rivulets in the soil which chokes the floor and ultimately
disappearing into an undetectable crevice. During past flood times, this leak
must have brought down enough sandy debris to choke the descending tunnel. There
are many large footprints in the sand, and the tracks seem to lead to a crack in
the avenue's east wall.
name: Avenue

desc:
A once-magnificent staircase leads upward and downward from this partly
collapsed chamber. From the centre of the polished marble floor of the hall's
west end, stone stairs ascend westward, then curve in mid-air toward the south
and disappear through the high ceiling. Huge boulders choke the east end of the
hall, but it is possible to follow the stairway downward and northward where it
curves under the fallen stone. The floor of the room is littered with shattered
ornamental stonework which has fallen from the ceiling. An archway is to be
found at the west end of this hall.
name: Stairwell

desc:
This great ten-sided hall, two hundred forty feet across, contains enough stone
benches and long tables to accommodate hundreds of dwarves. The walls are low
but the ceiling rises into a vaulted dome about sixty feet high. Ventilation
grates can be seen in the centre of the ceiling and in the walls. The rock of
the ceiling has been polished to highlight the contrast between the deep blue-
grey stone and a vein of mingled white and rose which branches from the south in
an intricate pattern. At the east end of the room, a pile of debris indicates
that the new lords of Moria have established a guardpost here.
name: Dining Hall

desc:
A great cast-iron stove dominates this room, stretching the entire length of the
north wall. The ash in its firebox suggests that it would burn either wood or
coal; but cobwebs and rats' nests testify that it has burned neither for many
long years. Cabinets along the south wall are looted and vacant. A trickle of
water dribbles from a pipe that emerges from the west wall, splashes into a
basin in the floor, and disappears down a drain.
name: Kitchen

desc:
A broad east-west corridor, whose walls are carved and inlaid in a geometric
pattern, and which has been built with the cross-section of a low, broad arch,
penetrates the stone here. There are no doors or side passages. A thin layer of
dust covers the floor; all is stillness and darkness.
name: East-West Passage

desc:
This tunnel, faced in precise but simple style with cream-coloured stone, leads
north and south. There are large cracks in the ceiling, walls, and floor,
evidence of the great tremors that have taken place in the heart of Zirakzigil.
name: Underground Corridor

desc:
You are on a curving stairway that ascends northward from here, curving to the
east. Below you, to the south, the passage has been completely blocked by a
great wall of stone. In some monstrous earthquake of the past, part of the
mountain's roots themselves have shifted, cutting off this passage as with a
knife's blade. The walls and the steps are themselves riven with cracks.
name: Stairwell

desc:
This room may once have been a bath or ceremonial pool. A trickle of clean water
runs from a spout in the shape of a dragon's head and pours into a stone
depression which occupies most of the rectangular high-ceilinged chamber. The
basin is full, now, of rotten stuff, remnants of carcasses, dead bats, bones,
and excreta. The stench is overpowering. Within the basin, you can hear
unpleasant slitherings and slurpings, as filth-eating creatures burrow and eat.
The drain of the pool has been blocked, and the overflow has flooded the floor
in the eastern part of the chamber with a stinking brown liquid.
name: Garbage Dump

desc:
This broad corridor is literally buried in a mixture of gravel and stinking
muck. The gravel has apparently been brought here in sacks and on litters, some
of which are lying here on the central pathway. The occupants of the ruined city
have apparently given this area over to the growing of some sort of fungus. A
trickle of stinking brown water enters the corridor from the west and flows
through furrows in the "soil".
name: Growing Chambers

desc:
The floor of this east-west corridor is largely buried in a sort of "soil" made
of gravel and foul refuse. The air is humid and heavy with the smell of waste.
Brown water dribbles down furrows from the west and percolates into the soil.
Once-handsome doors line the north and south walls of the passage, but as they
are hinged to open outward they cannot be budged due to the obstructing dirt.
name: Growing Chambers

desc:
This dark room, whose floor is awash in brown muck, contains ten stone
worktables, small even by the standards of Dwarvish furniture, each abutted by
very small seating-rails. A larger chair, now broken and overturned, stood near
the west end of the room. The walls were originally ornamented with murals of
Dwarven historical scenes, but the details have been scraped away or buried
under vile smearings. A line of runes can be dimly seen near the top of the
south wall.
name: Abandoned Hall

desc:
This part of the corridor is mostly unencumbered with gravel and muck, although
its smooth floor is slippery with muck which has been tracked in from the west.
Prints of boots and of naked, clawed feet are visible in the mud. The passage is
less ornate here than to the west, and it proceeds for a long distance without
encountering side passages or doors.
name: A Long Corridor

desc:
This is the northern end of a large storage area. A line of sturdy columns
supporting the ceiling runs north and south through its centre. Metal shelves
and bins line the walls. The floor is littered with trash - shards of pots,
rotten grain, bits of glass, empty flasks. Nothing of value can possibly remain
here, a thousand years after the dwarves who filled this room with their goods
were driven from their home.
name: Looted Storehouse

desc:
This is the southern end of a large storage area. Everything here has been
looted long ago. There are wet, muddy tracks on the floor, and a cooler, fresher
air blows from a ventilation grille high up on the southern wall. Overturned
shelves, upset bins, and smashed urns and flagons clutter the eastern side of
the chamber.
name: Looted Storehouse

desc:
This small passageway probably once led to the actual lodging areas of the
Naugrim, but the area to the south is now impassable and totally choked with
rubble. Whatever once lay beyond has been buried now for many centuries, and
will remain so until the dwarves return.
name: A Passageway

desc:
Once just a storage closet, this has been converted to something akin to a
grocery store. Messy shelves lined with stinking, rotting wares line the walls.
Piles of mildewed bread waste away on the floor. You shudder to think of how
long this stuff has been rotting here.
name: A Storage Room

desc:
You are halfway down a very slimy well. Algae and fungus float on top of the
water and stick to your clothes. You tread water to stay afloat and hope that
someone is at the top of the well to get you out!
name: Halfway down a well

desc:
This is the bottom of a well. The water here is over your head and you have to
hold your breath. Slime covers the walls, and the floor is scattered with
fragments of bones from unfortunate victims. The thick, slimy water sticks to
your skin and makes movement very difficult. Your only thought is to get out as
soon as possible!
name: At the Bottom of a Well

desc:
This underwater chamber was once an entryway, but the earthquakes of Khazad-dum
have changed it considerably. The passages that left east and south of here are
filled with rubble, and the wall to the west was broken and is now blocked by
loose rocks. Above you, a trapdoor can be accessed by a now rusted, and
un-needed, ladder.
name: A Hidden Alcove

desc:
This great crystal chamber was cut with great skill by some master enchanter.
The room is cut in a vaguely oval shape, with the crystals shaped to catch the
light and diffuse it into colourful rainbows. In the centre of the room, one
crystal pillar stands straight up. This pillar seems to radiate all of the light
in the room.
name: A Crystal Chamber

desc:
Although there is no dust here, no signs of any creature -- human, orc, or other
-- can be found. Everything in this chamber has been untouched for centuries or
longer. Hidden away for ages, it has housed its treasure since its inception.
name: Enclosed Chamber

desc:
This room is cut from hardened mud and limestone. Water dripping from the walls
and the ceiling indicates that a water source is nearby. The walls seem fragile
enough to break at any moment. There is a musty smell emanating from the chamber
above. There are coloured doors to the east and the west. To the north, an
exceedingly powerful field of magic blocks anything beyond it.
name: A Wet Intersection

desc:
This chamber is cut from a strange resonating crystal. Shaped like a huge bell,
every tiny sound echoes throughout the chamber, vibrating the strange crystal
formations. The vibrating crystals emit a lovely high-pitched musical sound. In
the very centre of the room, a crystal pillar stands up from the floor. This
pillar seems to be the source of the sound.
name: Musical Chamber

desc:
A ghastly stench fills the room as solid waste litters the northern end of the
room. Scraps and large chunks of rotting wood and other junk has also been
dumped here. Small holes in the ground serve as crude outhouses for elimination.
name: The Latrine
